The **2013 Brisbane Broncos season** was the 26th in [the club](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos")'s history. Coached by [Anthony Griffin](/wiki/Anthony_Griffin_(rugby_league) "Anthony Griffin (rugby league)") and captained by [Sam Thaiday](/wiki/Sam_Thaiday "Sam Thaiday"), they competed in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League")'s [2013 Telstra Premiership](/wiki/2013_NRL_season "2013 NRL season"). Brisbane finished the regular season in 12th (out of 16), thus failing to qualify for the finals for the first time since 2010.

According to a 2013 report, the Brisbane Broncos had the highest brand equity of any Australian sporting brand.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
